req.title: Upload onboarding document — variant 2
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three key dimensions: (1) allowed MIME types (PDF, PNG, JPG), (2) a hard size ceiling of 10 MB, and (3) idempotency under retry. Equivalence Partitioning yields valid-type and invalid-type partitions. Boundary Value Analysis targets exactly 10 MB (on-boundary), 10 MB+1 byte (over-boundary), and a minimal 1-byte file. A Decision Table covers the cross-product of size × MIME. Negative Path covers each error code (413, 415). State Transition Coverage handles the retry/idempotency contract. The suite is kept to six focused tests that together satisfy the minimum three (positive, negative, boundary) while providing fuller coverage. Each test uses lightweight fixtures and helper placeholders to stay within the 40-line limit.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_returns_200
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
A well-formed PDF file of 1 MB exists at <valid_pdf_path>
actions: POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the 1 MB PDF file
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a document ID
trace: R-WEB-078: file <= 10 MB must be accepted
R-WEB-078: virus scan must be queued on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_returns_200(auth_session, tmp_pdf_1mb):
    url = auth_session.base_url + "/documents/upload"
    with open(tmp_pdf_1mb, "rb") as fh:
        resp = auth_session.post(
            url,
            files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", fh, "application/pdf")},
        )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "Missing document_id in response"
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued", "Virus scan not queued"

tests.name: test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
A PDF file of exactly 10 MB + 1 byte exists at <oversized_pdf_path>
actions: POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the oversized PDF file
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing file size limit
trace: R-WEB-078: files outside the 10 MB limit must be rejected
R-WEB-078: error pathway 413 when size > 10 MB
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413(auth_session, tmp_file_10mb_plus_1):
    url = auth_session.base_url + "/documents/upload"
    with open(tmp_file_10mb_plus_1, "rb") as fh:
        resp = auth_session.post(
            url,
            files={"file": ("big.pdf", fh, "application/pdf")},
        )
    assert resp.status_code == 413, f"Expected 413, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Missing error detail in body"

tests.name: test_upload_file_at_exact_10mb_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
A PDF file of exactly 10 485 760 bytes (10 MiB) exists at <boundary_pdf_path>
actions: POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the exactly-10-MB PDF file
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a document ID
trace: R-WEB-078: files up to 10 MB must be accepted (boundary inclusive)
R-WEB-078: error pathway 413 applies only when size > 10 MB
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

TEN_MB = 10 * 1024 * 1024

def test_upload_file_at_exact_10mb_boundary_is_accepted(auth_session, tmp_path):
    boundary_file = tmp_path / "boundary.pdf"
    boundary_file.write_bytes(b"%PDF-1.4\n" + b"0" * (TEN_MB - 9))
    assert boundary_file.stat().st_size == TEN_MB
    url = auth_session.base_url + "/documents/upload"
    with open(boundary_file, "rb") as fh:
        resp = auth_session.post(
            url,
            files={"file": ("boundary.pdf", fh, "application/pdf")},
        )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Boundary file rejected with {resp.status_code}"
    assert "document_id" in resp.json(), "Missing document_id at boundary"

tests.name: test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
A small GIF file (not in allow-list) exists or is generated in-memory
actions: POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with a GIF file declared as image/gif
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing unsupported media type
trace: R-WEB-078: allowed formats are PDF, PNG, JPG only
R-WEB-078: error pathway 415 when MIME not in allow-list
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
import requests

GIF_HEADER = b"GIF89a\x01\x00\x01\x00\x00\xff\x00,\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x01\x00\x00\x02\x00;"

def test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415(auth_session):
    url = auth_session.base_url + "/documents/upload"
    resp = auth_session.post(
        url,
        files={"file": ("sneaky.gif", io.BytesIO(GIF_HEADER), "image/gif")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 415, f"Expected 415, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Missing error detail for 415"

tests.name: test_upload_png_and_jpg_are_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
Minimal valid PNG and JPG byte sequences available via <minimal_png_bytes> and <minimal_jpg_bytes>
trace: R-WEB-078: PNG and JPG are explicitly allowed formats
R-WEB-078: document stored and virus scan queued for all valid types
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
import requests

MINIMAL_PNG = bytes.fromhex(
    "89504e470d0a1a0a0000000d49484452000000010000000108000000003a7e9b55"
    "0000000a49444154789c6260000000020001e221bc330000000049454e44ae426082"
)
MINIMAL_JPG = bytes.fromhex("ffd8ffe000104a46494600010100000100010000ffdb004300"
    "08060606070605080707070909080a0c140d0c0b0b0c1912130f141d1a1f1e1d"
    "1a1c1c20242e2720222c231c1c2837292c30313434341f27393d38323c2e3334"
    "32ffc0000b080001000101011100ffc4001f000001050101010101000000000000"
    "00000102030405060708090a0bffda00080101000003f0ffd9")

@pytest.mark.parametrize("fname,data,mime", [
    ("doc.png", MINIMAL_PNG, "image/png"),
    ("doc.jpg", MINIMAL_JPG, "image/jpeg"),
])
def test_upload_png_and_jpg_are_accepted(auth_session, fname, data, mime):
    url = auth_session.base_url + "/documents/upload"
    resp = auth_session.post(url, files={"file": (fname, io.BytesIO(data), mime)})
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"{mime} rejected: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued"

tests.name: test_upload_is_idempotent_under_retry
preconditions: User is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
A valid 1 MB PDF file exists at <valid_pdf_path>
The API supports an idempotency key header (e.g. X-Idempotency-Key)
actions: POST the same PDF file with identical X-Idempotency-Key header twice
Capture both response statuses and document IDs
expected: Both responses return the same document_id
Second request does not create a duplicate document
trace: R-WEB-078: action must be idempotent under retry
R-WEB-078: variant 2 additional semantics — retry safety
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
import requests

def test_upload_is_idempotent_under_retry(auth_session, tmp_pdf_1mb):
    url = auth_session.base_url + "/documents/upload"
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {"X-Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    def do_upload():
        with open(tmp_pdf_1mb, "rb") as fh:
            return auth_session.post(
                url,
                files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", fh, "application/pdf")},
                headers=headers,
            )
    resp1 = do_upload()
    resp2 = do_upload()
    assert resp1.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 201)
    doc_id_1 = resp1.json().get("document_id")
    doc_id_2 = resp2.json().get("document_id")
    assert doc_id_1 == doc_id_2, f"Idempotency broken: {doc_id_1} != {doc_id_2}"
